page: Microsoft Azure E96a_v4 is an EAv4-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7452 processor operating at 3.35 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 32 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 16 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 672.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 2458 GB of local storage. No GPU resources are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is categorized in the bottom 10%, and single-threaded gzip and bzip3 operations fall in the middle 50%. However, multi-threaded performance is robust, with bzip3 multi-threaded decompression and Passmark CPU scores ranking in the top 25%. Notably, the Passmark CPU encryption test achieves top-tier status in the top 10%. Memory performance is also strong, with cached small-block read-only memory bandwidth in the top 25%, while uncached large-block read and write bandwidths remain in the middle 50%.

This server is configured for memory-intensive workloads that benefit from high parallelization rather than raw single-core speed. Tradeoffs include lower single-core efficiency and poor performance on large-scale LLM inference, such as prompt processing for 70B models which falls in the bottom 10%. Conversely, it demonstrates strong capabilities in software compilation with Clang, ray tracing, and Redis SET operations, which all score in the top 25%. The high memory capacity and substantial local storage make it suitable for relational databases, in-memory caching layers, and multi-threaded data processing pipelines.
description: Microsoft Azure E96a_v4 is an EAv4-family virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 processor with 96 dedicated vCPUs and 48 physical cores running at 3.35 GHz. It features 672.0 GB of RAM, 2458 GB of local storage, and no GPU accelerators. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance exhibits a distinct performance profile: while single-core CPU benchmarks rank in the bottom 10%, multi-threaded capabilities are strong, with Passmark CPU and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression scores in the top 25%. The system also achieves top-tier performance in CPU encryption tests. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is highly efficient, though large uncached blocks deliver average throughput. This resource density makes the instance qualitatively cost-effective for memory-heavy, highly parallelized workloads such as database operations, software compilation, and Redis caching, though it is less suited for single-threaded tasks or large LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96a_v4 is an EAv4-family VM with 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage, delivering strong multi-threaded and encryption performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96a_v4 features 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage, optimized for multi-threaded and memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity virtual machine featuring ninety-six AMD vCPUs and substantial memory, optimized for parallel processing and memory-intensive database workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 processor.
672.0 GB of system memory paired with 2458 GB of local storage.
Top-tier CPU encryption performance and strong multi-threaded decompression capabilities.
Strong memory bandwidth for cached small-block read operations.
Lower single-core performance and limited efficiency for large LLM inference.
Well-suited for databases, Redis caching, and parallel software compilation.
